text:
Are Ordinarily Self-Administered and Do 
Not Require the Skills of a Licensed Nurse to Be Provided Safely and Effectively 

NOTE: Prefilling of insulin syringes is ordinarily performed by the diabetic as part of 
the self-administration of the insulin and, unlike the injection of the insulin, does not 
require the skill of a licensed nurse to be performed properly. Therefore, if HHA staff 
performs the prefilling of insulin syringes, it is considered to be a home health aide 
service. However,
